inferred_domain: Clinical hepatology / survival analysis of primary biliary cirrhosis (PBC) patients, derived from the Mayo Clinic 1974-1984 randomized placebo-controlled D-penicillamine trial; the task is multiclass prediction of patient outcome (alive/censored, transplanted, or dead) from baseline demographics, physical exam findings, and liver-function laboratory values.

column_semantics.N_Days: Follow-up time in days from study registration (baseline) to the recorded endpoint event (death, liver transplant) or to the last contact at which the patient was still alive/censored.
column_semantics.Drug: Randomized treatment assignment in the trial, either the experimental copper-chelating agent D-penicillamine or Placebo.
column_semantics.Age: Patient age expressed in DAYS at study registration (divide by ~365.25 to obtain years, typically ~26-78 years).
column_semantics.Sex: Biological sex of the patient (M = male, F = female; PBC is strongly female-predominant, so most rows are F).
column_semantics.Ascites: Presence of ascites, pathological fluid accumulation in the abdominal cavity, a sign of decompensated end-stage liver disease (Y/N).
column_semantics.Hepatomegaly: Presence of an enlarged liver detected on physical examination (Y/N).
column_semantics.Spiders: Presence of spider angiomata, cutaneous vascular lesions associated with chronic liver disease (Y/N).
column_semantics.Edema: Severity of peripheral edema coded N = no edema/no diuretics, S = edema without diuretics or resolved by diuretics, Y = edema persisting despite diuretics (worst prognosis).
column_semantics.Bilirubin: Serum total bilirubin in mg/dL; the single strongest prognostic lab in PBC, rising as the liver loses excretory capacity.
column_semantics.Cholesterol: Serum total cholesterol in mg/dL, often elevated in cholestatic liver disease.
column_semantics.Albumin: Serum albumin in g/dL, a marker of hepatic synthetic function that FALLS as liver function deteriorates (lower = worse).
column_semantics.Copper: Urine copper in ug/day; accumulates due to impaired biliary copper excretion, tracking cholestasis severity.
column_semantics.Alk_Phos: Serum alkaline phosphatase in U/L, a cholestatic enzyme markedly elevated in PBC due to bile-duct injury.
column_semantics.SGOT: Serum glutamic-oxaloacetic transaminase (AST) in U/mL, a hepatocellular injury enzyme.
column_semantics.Tryglicerides: Serum triglycerides in mg/dL, a lipid measure altered in cholestatic states.
column_semantics.Platelets: Platelet count (~x1000/uL); falls with portal hypertension and hypersplenism (lower = more advanced disease).
column_semantics.Prothrombin: Prothrombin time in seconds; prolongs as the failing liver produces fewer clotting factors (higher = worse).
column_semantics.Stage: Histologic stage of disease (1-4) from liver biopsy reflecting fibrosis, where 4 indicates established cirrhosis (higher = more advanced).

known_relationships: Cholestatic cluster co-moves upward: Bilirubin, Alk_Phos, urine Copper, and Cholesterol rise as bile flow is obstructed; Bilirubin and Copper are especially tied to prognosis.
Hepatic synthetic-failure cluster: low Albumin and prolonged Prothrombin both signal failing synthetic function and co-occur in advanced disease.
Portal-hypertension cluster: low Platelets, Ascites, Edema, and Spiders co-occur and mark decompensation; Ascites + diuretic-resistant Edema are the most ominous signs.
Histologic Stage 4 correlates with decompensation signs (high Bilirubin, low Platelets, low Albumin, Ascites).
These columns are the exact inputs to the validated Mayo PBC risk score and MELD-type scores (Bilirubin, Albumin, Prothrombin, Edema), known to predict death/transplant.
Higher Bilirubin, prolonged Prothrombin, low Albumin, Ascites/Edema, advanced Stage, and older Age push outcome toward D (death) or CL (transplant).
Drug effect is clinically weak: the original trial concluded D-penicillamine did NOT improve survival, so Drug is a low-signal feature.
external_data_candidates: UCI ML Repository ID 878 'Cirrhosis Patient Survival Prediction' (https://archive.ics.uci.edu/dataset/878): the exact 418-patient original table this competition was synthesized from; identical schema. Appending its rows to train with an is_original flag is a standard winning tactic for S3E26.
R survival package 'pbc' (n=418): canonical academic source, maps column-for-column.
leakage_risks: N_Days is follow-up duration mechanically entangled with the outcome, but it is provided in BOTH train and test so it must be used; treat as outcome-correlated and validate carefully.
id is a meaningless row index; never feed it as a feature.
When appending original UCI rows, verify no near-duplicate original rows leak test answers; keep augmented rows out of validation folds.
high_value_features: Mayo PBC / MELD-like prognostic index from log(Bilirubin), Albumin, log(Prothrombin), Age, Edema.
Log-transform right-skewed labs (Bilirubin, Alk_Phos, SGOT, Copper, Cholesterol, Tryglicerides).
Decompensation count: sum of Ascites=Y, Hepatomegaly=Y, Spiders=Y, Edema in {S,Y}.
Edema ordinal N<S<Y and diuretic-resistant binary.
Cholestasis composite from standardized Bilirubin + Alk_Phos + Copper.
Stage x lab interactions (Stage4 flag interacted with Bilirubin/Platelets).
